    Part 1: Immunology Foundations

    What is the immune system?

    How does the immune system recognize and attack threats?

    Tolerance: how does your body learn not to attack itself?

    [Replace with content: central and peripheral tolerance, regulatory T cells, why this matters.]

    Part 2: The Immunological Puzzle of Pregnancy

    Why pregnancy is immunologically paradoxical

    [Replace with content: the fetus carries paternal antigens and is technically “foreign” — so why isn’t it rejected?]

    The uterus is an immune organ

    [Replace with content: the decidua, uterine NK cells, macrophages, and how they create an immune-privileged environment.]

    How the placenta actively negotiates with the immune system

    [Replace with content: trophoblast invasion, HLA-G expression, cytokine signaling at the maternal-fetal interface.]

    The immune system shifts across trimesters

    [Replace with content: pro-inflammatory implantation → immune tolerance mid-pregnancy → pro-inflammatory labor.]

    Part 3 — Open Questions & Cutting-Edge Research

    When it goes wrong

    [Replace with content: preeclampsia, recurrent miscarriage, preterm birth — what we know about immune dysregulation in each.]

    Cool research frontiers

    [Replace with content: the pregnancy microbiome, fetal microchimerism (fetal cells persisting in the mother), COVID-19 and vaccines during pregnancy.]
